properties.remark: SEVERAL HOUSES EXPERIENCED THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE IN THE MEREAUX AREA...PRIMARILY ALONG JUDY DRIVE. DAMAGE WAS MAINLY TO ROOFING MATERIAL REMOVED...A FEW WINDOWS BLOWN OUT...A DOOR BLOWN IN...AND FENCES BLOWN DOWN. A FEW LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE ALSO SNAPPED.
SEVERAL HOUSES IN THE VIOLET AREA...PRIMARILY ALONG 6TH STREET...EXPERIENCED THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE. PRIMARY IMPACT WAS SHINGLES...AND ROOFING MATERIAL REMOVED...AND FENCES WERE DOWNED.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE VICNITY OF MS HIGHWAY 48 AND PIGOT EASTERLING ROAD. SEVERAL LARGE PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED...AND LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED. A HOUSE ROOF WAS DAMAGED BY A FALLEN TREE. DAMAGE PATH 0.50 MILE. PATH WIDTH 40 YARDS. INTENSITY EF0. ESTIMATED MAX WIND 85 MPH. TIME ESIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
FLOODING WAS REPORTED AT TWO BUSINESSES AT BALWIN STREET AND SOUTH LAMAR STREET.
A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN WEST FELCIANA PARISH.
EIGHT FOOT BOX CULVERT WASHED OUT ON DAVID ALLEN...JOE SMITH ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED
SEVERAL HOMES IN NORTHERN ST HELENA PARISH HAD MINOR FLOODING. TIME ESTIMATED
AN ELDERLY COUPLE WAS RESCUED FROM FLOOD WATERS IN THE FERNWOOD FLATS AREA.

SEVERAL PINE TREES AND LARGE LIVE OAK BRANCHES DOWN IN RIVER OAKS SUBDIVISION ALONG INDIAN VILLAGE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SEVERAL HOMES IN SILVER CREEK AREA OF NORTHERN WILKINSON COUNTY HAD SIGNIFICANT FLOODING DUE TO RAPIDLY RISING WATER. 5 PEOPLE WERE TRAPPED BY FLOOD WATERS AND RESCUED AROUND 815AM.
COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS SIGNIFICANT FLOODING ONGOING IN THE COMMUNITY OF CROSBY. MANY ROADS FLOODED AND ESTIMATED 20 TO 30 HOMES HAVE SOME TYPE OF FLOODING.

WIDESPREAD STREET FLOODING...SOME SECTIONS OF ROADS IMPASSABLE TO LOW PROFILE VEHICLES DUE TO ONE TO TWO FEET OF WATER...ACROSS SLIDELL. SOME VEHICLES WERE STRANDED ACCORDING TO TELEVISION...RADIO AND PRINT MEDIA AND SOCIAL MEDIA SITES.

OLDER MODEL SINGLE WIDE TRAILER SUSTAINED MODERATE DAMAGE FROM APPARENT REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT EVENT ON OLD JOE MORAN ROAD NEAR KILN. FRONT PORCH DISLOCATED AND BLOWN OVER TRAILER. DEBRIS BLOWN 50-100 YDS DOWNRANGE. TWO PINE TREES SNAPPED AT 15FT ACROSS ROAD WEST OF TRAILER. CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE INTERIOR WITH OBJECTS LODGED IN THE WALLS. ESTIMATED 65-70 MPH. TRAILER STRAPPED BUT MOVED 6-8 INCHES ON CINDERS. ALL WINDOWS BROKEN.
*** 1 INJ *** EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN IN AND AROUND MCCOMB. ONE TREE FELL ONTO AN APARTMENT BUILDING DAMAGING IT AND INJURING AN OCCUPANT.

*** 2 INJ *** SURVEYORS HAVE DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 75-80 MPH STRUCK THE SANTA ROSA RV RESORT MARINA AT 5800 MOSS BLUFF ROAD. 20 RV TRAILERS WERE OVERTURNED...ONE ROLLED UPHILL. SEVERAL TREES TOPPED ALONG WITH LARGE LIVE OAK LIMBS BROKEN. TWO STUCCO VENEER WALLS ON AN APARTMENT STRUCTURE COLLAPSED. TWO INDIVIDUALS WERE EXTRACTED FROM ONE TRAILER THAT WAS DESTROYED AND WERE HOSPITALIZED FOR MODERATE INJURIES. TIME ESTIMATED BY KLIX AND KMOB RADAR SRM DATA.

HEAVY RAINFALL RESULTED IN SIGNIFICANT ROADWAY FLOODING. APPROXIMATELY 13 HOUSES...AND SEVERAL BUSINESSES AND GOVERNMENT BUILDINGS HAD MINOR FLOODING. FLOOD REPORTS WERE FROM VARIOUS LOCATIONS IN THE PARISH.
